message:
docs: Document uv + CPython 3.13 as the standard Python toolchain

This commit adds comprehensive documentation for the new Python tooling
setup based on uv (fast Python package manager) and CPython 3.13, which
was established in commit 7052486.

Changes Overview
----------------
1. README.md: New "Python Tooling" section (29 lines)
2. test-suite/README.md: New prerequisite section (17 lines)

Both documents explain setup, usage, and integration with existing workflows.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1. README.md Changes
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Added Section: "🛠 Python Tooling (uv + CPython 3.13)"

Location: Immediately after Quick Start, before "The Problem" section
Rationale: Users need to know about Python setup before running tests

Content Structure
-----------------
1. **Context statement**: Explains that uv + CPython 3.13 is the single
   source of truth for all Python code in the project

2. **Setup instructions**: 4-step process
   ```bash
   # 1) Install uv (one-time)
   curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

   # 2) Create managed environment
   uv sync --python 3.13

   # 3) Activate environment
   source .venv/bin/activate

   # 4) Run Python tools
   uv run python test-suite/run_manifest.py --case js-core-buggy
   ```

3. **Integration note**: Explains how shell scripts automatically pick
   up `.venv/bin/python3` when environment is activated

Key Messages
------------
✓ uv + CPython 3.13 is the standard (not system Python)
✓ pyproject.toml + uv.lock are single source of truth
✓ Works seamlessly with existing shell scripts
✓ .venv/bin/python3 is automatically used when activated

Why This Matters
----------------
- **Consistency**: Everyone uses same Python version (3.13)
- **Speed**: uv is 10-100x faster than pip
- **Reliability**: Lockfile ensures reproducible environments
- **Simplicity**: One command (uv sync) sets everything up

What This Covers
----------------
The new section explicitly mentions which components use Python:
- Manifest runner (run_manifest.py)
- Test fixtures (where applicable)
- Inline analyzers inside language modules

This gives users complete picture of Python usage in the project.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
2. test-suite/README.md Changes
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Added Section: "Prerequisite: uv + CPython 3.13"

Location: Beginning of "🧪 Running Tests" section
Rationale: Must set up environment before running any tests

Content Structure
-----------------
1. **Lockfile reference**: Points to pyproject.toml/uv.lock as truth

2. **Quick setup**: Minimal steps to get started
   ```bash
   cd ultimate_bug_scanner
   uv sync --python 3.13
   source .venv/bin/activate

   # Verify with smoke test
   uv run python test-suite/run_manifest.py --case js-core-buggy
   ```

3. **Usage assumption**: States that all subsequent examples assume
   `.venv/bin` is on PATH (via activation)

Why Here?
---------
- Test-suite specific documentation
- First thing users need before running tests
- Provides concrete verification command (smoke test)
- Sets expectations for all following examples

Integration with Existing Docs
-------------------------------
The new section flows naturally:
1. Fixture organization (existing)
2. **Prerequisite: uv setup (NEW)**
3. Scan individual buggy files (existing)
4. Run manifest tests (existing)

Users hit the prerequisite before seeing test commands, preventing
"python3: command not found" or version mismatch errors.

Examples of Commands Now Documented
------------------------------------
Before this commit:
```bash
python3 ./run_manifest.py --case js-core-buggy  # Which python3?
```

After this commit (user knows to):
```bash
# Option 1: Activate first
source .venv/bin/activate
python test-suite/run_manifest.py --case js-core-buggy

# Option 2: Use uv run
uv run python test-suite/run_manifest.py --case js-core-buggy
```

Both approaches now clearly documented with full context.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Cross-Document Consistency
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Both documents use consistent terminology:
- "uv + CPython 3.13" (not just "Python" or "uv")
- "pyproject.toml + uv.lock" (emphasizing both files)
- ".venv/bin" (specific path, not abstract)
- "single source of truth" (philosophical alignment)

Both use consistent commands:
- `uv sync --python 3.13` (explicit version)
- `source .venv/bin/activate` (POSIX shell)
- `uv run python <script>` (alternative to activation)

Both reference the same workflow:
1. Install uv (one-time)
2. Sync environment
3. Activate or use uv run
4. Run Python tools

This consistency ensures users get the same message regardless of which
README they read first.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
User Experience Improvements
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Before this commit:
- No mention of Python version requirements
- No guidance on environment setup
- Users might use system Python (wrong version)
- Potential version mismatch errors

After this commit:
- Clear Python version requirement (3.13)
- Step-by-step setup instructions
- Two usage patterns documented (activate vs uv run)
- Verification command provided (smoke test)

Error Prevention
----------------
Common errors this documentation prevents:

1. **Wrong Python version**:
   - Before: User runs with Python 3.8, gets syntax errors
   - After: Documented requirement for 3.13, uv enforces it

2. **Missing dependencies**:
   - Before: User runs script, imports fail
   - After: `uv sync` installs all dependencies first

3. **Environment confusion**:
   - Before: Multiple Python installs, wrong one used
   - After: .venv/bin takes precedence when activated

4. **Inconsistent environments**:
   - Before: Different dependency versions across machines
   - After: uv.lock ensures identical environments

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Implementation Notes
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Placement Decisions
-------------------
1. **Main README.md**: After Quick Start
   - Users have installed UBS, now need Python setup
   - Before detailed problem/solution sections
   - High visibility, can't be missed

2. **test-suite/README.md**: Before test instructions
   - Prerequisite for everything that follows
   - Natural flow: setup → usage
   - Prevents "cart before horse" problems

Formatting Choices
------------------
- Emoji headers (🛠, 🧪) match existing style
- Code blocks use bash syntax highlighting
- Commands include comments for clarity
- Step numbering for sequential processes
- ℹ️ callout boxes for important notes

Link Strategy
-------------
- Links to uv GitHub repo for credibility
- Links to Astral (uv installer) for setup
- No deep links to docs (they change)
- Self-contained instructions (no hunting)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Related Work
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

This documentation builds on:
- Commit 7052486: Added pyproject.toml + uv.lock
- Commit 7323e37: Added .venv/ to .gitignore

Together, these form complete Python tooling story:
1. Project files (pyproject.toml, uv.lock) - defines environment
2. Gitignore (.gitignore) - excludes generated files
3. Documentation (this commit) - explains how to use it

Next likely changes:
- run_all.sh updated to prefer `uv run` (coming in next commit)
- CI/CD integration with uv (future work)
- Additional Python dependencies as needed (when added)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Impact Summary
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Documentation Quality
----------------------
+ Clear setup instructions for Python tooling
+ Consistent messaging across both READMEs
+ Prevents common environment issues
+ Provides verification steps (smoke tests)

User Onboarding
----------------
+ New contributors know exact Python setup
+ Existing users can migrate to uv smoothly
+ Clear migration path from system Python
+ Fast setup (one uv sync command)

Project Maturity
-----------------
+ Professional Python tooling (uv is industry-leading)
+ Modern Python version (3.13 is latest stable)
+ Reproducible environments (lockfile)
+ Speed benefits (10-100x faster than pip)

No Functional Changes
----------------------
This is pure documentation - no code behavior changes. Existing Python
scripts work unchanged; this just documents how to run them properly.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -19,6 +19,29 @@ curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_bug_scan
 
 ---
 
+## 🛠 Python Tooling (uv + CPython 3.13)
+
+All helper scripts (manifest runner, fixtures, inline analyzers inside `ubs`) now assume a single source of truth: **CPython 3.13 managed by [uv](https://github.com/astral-sh/uv)** living inside `.venv/` at the repo root.
+
+```bash
+# 1) Install uv (one-time)
+curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
+
+# 2) Create the managed environment defined by pyproject.toml / uv.lock
+uv sync --python 3.13
+
+# 3) Activate it whenever you work in this repo (puts .venv/bin first on PATH)
+source .venv/bin/activate
+
+# 4) Run any Python entrypoint through the env
+uv run python test-suite/run_manifest.py --case js-core-buggy
+# ...or rely on 'python'/'python3' now that they point at .venv/bin/python3.13
+```
+
+> ℹ️ Shell scripts that invoke `python3` (language modules under `modules/`, `test-suite/run_all.sh`, etc.) automatically pick up `.venv/bin/python3` as long as the environment is activated or `.venv/bin` is on your `PATH`. The pinned `pyproject.toml` + `uv.lock` are the single source of truth for this toolchain.
+
+---
+

 ## 🧪 Running Tests
 
+### Prerequisite: uv + CPython 3.13
+
+The repo ships a `pyproject.toml`/`uv.lock` pair that pins us to Python 3.13. Sync the environment once and either activate `.venv` or wrap commands with `uv run`:
+
+```bash
+cd ultimate_bug_scanner
+uv sync --python 3.13
+source .venv/bin/activate  # or: uv run python <command>
+
+# smoke-test the manifest runner with the pinned interpreter
+uv run python test-suite/run_manifest.py --case js-core-buggy
+```
+
+All subsequent examples below assume that `.venv/bin` is on your `PATH` so `python`/`python3` resolve to that managed interpreter.
+
 ### Scan Individual Buggy Files
 
 ```bash
